search_terms_used: hydration running performance dehydration endurance systematic review PubMed
hyponatremia marathon runners sodium risk factors
drinking to thirst vs scheduled drinking endurance exercise
sweat rate sodium running individual variation personalized hydration
electrolyte supplementation endurance running performance sodium ingestion
coverage_notes: Strong coverage across the domain. Includes 1 meta-analysis (dehydration vs time-trial performance), 1 systematic review with meta-analysis (dehydration and RPE), 1 international consensus statement (exercise-associated hyponatremia), 1 landmark cohort study (hyponatremia incidence in marathon runners), 1 methodological review challenging dehydration/performance assumptions, 1 narrative review comparing drinking strategies, 1 review on sweat rate/sodium variability methodology, and 1 prospective observational study on sodium supplementation in ultramarathons. Covers dehydration thresholds, hyponatremia risk factors, drink-to-thirst vs scheduled drinking, sweat sodium variability, and sodium supplementation efficacy. Populations range from recreational marathon runners to ultra-endurance athletes. Gap: no RCT specifically on recreational runners in temperate conditions.

papers.title: Effect of exercise-induced dehydration on time-trial exercise performance: a meta-analysis
papers.notes: Meta-analysis quantifying the effect of exercise-induced dehydration on self-paced time-trial endurance performance across multiple studies.

papers.title: Impact of dehydration on perceived exertion during endurance exercise: A systematic review with meta-analysis
papers.notes: Systematic review with meta-analysis examining whether exercise-induced dehydration meaningfully increases ratings of perceived exertion during endurance exercise.

papers.title: Does Hypohydration Really Impair Endurance Performance? Methodological Considerations for Interpreting Hydration Research
papers.notes: Methodological review examining how blinding failures, dehydration protocols, and study designs may have confounded prior research on dehydration and endurance performance.

papers.title: Hyponatremia among runners in the Boston Marathon
papers.notes: Prospective cohort study estimating hyponatremia incidence and identifying risk factors among 2002 Boston Marathon runners.

papers.title: Statement of the Third International Exercise-Associated Hyponatremia Consensus Development Conference, Carlsbad, California, 2015
papers.notes: International expert consensus statement on the diagnosis, prevention, and treatment of exercise-associated hyponatremia, replacing the second consensus statement.

papers.title: Drinking Strategies: Planned Drinking Versus Drinking to Thirst
papers.notes: Narrative review comparing planned/scheduled fluid intake versus ad libitum thirst-driven drinking during exercise of varying duration and intensity.

papers.title: Sweating Rate and Sweat Sodium Concentration in Athletes: A Review of Methodology and Intra/Interindividual Variability
papers.notes: Methodological review examining sources of intra- and inter-individual variability in sweating rate and sweat sodium concentration during exercise.

papers.title: Prospective Observational Study of Weight-based Assessment of Sodium Supplements on Ultramarathon Performance (WASSUP)
papers.notes: Prospective observational study examining whether ad libitum sodium supplementation affects pace, finish time, or sodium balance during multi-stage ultramarathons.
